assertions.title: Server unreachable error handling in TUI

steps.action: Start server on port 9849 and confirm health.
steps.observed: curl http://127.0.0.1:9849/health returned 200 with status ok.

steps.action: Launch TUI via tuistory and capture initial state.
steps.observed: InboxApp rendered successfully in terminal session.

steps.action: Induce outage by killing process listening on 9849, then trigger retry with Ctrl+R.
steps.expected: TUI should show clear unreachable status and stay alive.
steps.observed: Status bar showed 'Server unreachable — press Ctrl+R to retry' and app continued running.

steps.action: Keep outage active for sustained period (>30s threshold) and re-capture state.
steps.expected: No crash during sustained outage; persistent unreachable status remains visible.
steps.observed: After 35s outage wait, TUI clock advanced and status still showed the same unreachable message without exit/crash.

steps.action: Supplemental: restart server and retry refresh.
steps.observed: Server health returned to 200; TUI stayed alive on retry, though status text remained 'Server unreachable — press Ctrl+R to retry'.

evidence.network: Health checks: pre-outage 200, post-kill 000, post-restart 200 (curl against 127.0.0.1:9849/health).

frictions.description: During supplemental recovery check, status bar remained on unreachable message even after server restart and manual Ctrl+R retries.
frictions.resolution: Not resolved in this validation run; recorded as supplemental observation.

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-HARD-008): pass. Under sustained server outage, the TUI remained alive and displayed a clear unreachable status message instead of crashing. Supplemental recovery showed server health restored, but status text did not clear during this run.
